subject: Re: Where are my MIPs?
body:
I see. I think I read too quickly :)

What you see with Relion 4 with diminishing density along Z is the same as
what I normally see. I did a few tests with Relion4 vs. Warp/Relion3 before
and none of them would give different results in terms of density,
especially MIPs.

Best,
H

On Mon, Jun 5, 2023 at 11:49 AM Ricardo Righetto <ricardorighetto@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Hi Huy,
>
> If I understand you correctly, this is exactly what I did. The first two
> screenshots I sent are from the reconstructions in Relion3/4 before any
> alignment.
>
> Thank you,
>
> --
> Ricardo Diogo Righetto
>
> Em seg., 5 de jun. de 2023 17:31, Huy Bui <huybk81@gmail.com> escreveu:
>
>> Hi Ricardo,
>>
>> You should just did a reconstruction (without alignment) of the subtomo
>> in Relion 4 or 3 to see whether the reconstruction have the MIPs. CTF
>> should not affect the MIPs.
>>
>> H
>>
>> On Mon, Jun 5, 2023 at 11:01 AM Ricardo Righetto <
>> ricardorighetto@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi everyone,
>>>
>>> After obtaining a nice average in STOPGAP, we are trying to push the
>>> resolution of the microtubule inner proteins (MIPs) in our map. For this
>>> I'm trying both Relion4 and Warp-Relion3-M pipelines.
>>> I import the set of particle coordinates and angles determined from
>>> STOPGAP into Relion4 or Warp, where I reconstruct subtomograms.
>>> One thing that is intriguing me is that the initial reconstructions I
>>> get from each pipeline look very different from each other (Relion4 is
>>> gray, Warp+Relion3 is yellow):
>>>
>>> [image: RELION-4_vs_Warp_RELION-3_front.png]
>>> [image: RELION-4_vs_Warp_RELION-3_side.png]
>>>
>>> The reconstruction from Warp-extracted subtomos (yellow) does seem to
>>> have more uniform resolution throughout the box (whereas the Relion4
>>> reconstruction vanishes along Z) and is less noisy, however, it is
>>> completely devoid of MIPs?!?
>>>
>>> My initial thought was that I might be importing the wrong set of
>>> particles, but after cross-checking several times, I am sure that I am
>>> reconstructing the correct particles. Everything is exactly the same in
>>> both pipelines, except that in Warp I reestimated the defocus of the tilts,
>>> whereas Relion4 takes whatever we estimated with CTFFIND4 earlier. Could
>>> just this defocus (re)estimation change the appearance of the map in such a
>>> way?
>>> I understand that Warp might filter and normalize subtomos in a
>>> different way than Relion4, but still, how can the initial reconstructions
>>> using the same coordinates and same angles look so different?
>>>
>>> Furthermore, after running 3D auto-refine in both pipelines (Relion4 vs
>>> Relion3), the Warp subtomos in Relion3 converge to better global resolution
>>> (20 vs 24 A), but the map still does not show the MIPs, whereas the Relion4
>>> refined average, albeit lower in resolution, looks more like what I'd
>>> expect (again, Relion4 is gray, Warp+Relion3 is yellow):
>>>
>>> [image: RELION-4_refined_orientations.png]
>>> [image: Warp_RELION-3_refined_orientations.png]
>>>
>>> Interestingly, and I don't know if/how it's related, the Warp subtomos
>>> in Relion3 tend to stick to the orientation of the filament (sharper red
>>> peaks in the Euler histogram), whereas in Relion4 many subtomograms seem to
>>> drift away during refinement (lots of blue dots), which might explain the
>>> difference in resolution.
>>>
>>> Has anyone observed this behavior before, or has any thoughts what might
>>> be going on?
>>>
>>> Thanks in advance!
>>>
>>> Best wishes,
>>>
>>> --
>>> Ricardo Diogo Righetto
>>>
>>> --
>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google
>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send
>>> an email to warp-em+unsubscribe@googlegroups.com.
>>> To view this discussion on the web visit
>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/CAB7-Rrvh0v_SzuH50rX40P0P2yA-fL14ayn3NU7EhMyGKQdXQg%40mail.gmail.com
>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/CAB7-Rrvh0v_SzuH50rX40P0P2yA-fL14ayn3NU7EhMyGKQdXQg%40mail.gmail.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>> .
>>>
>>
